chokepoints.name: Suez Canal / Red Sea route exposure
chokepoints.region: Egypt / Red Sea / Mediterranean-Indian Ocean corridor
chokepoints.typical_planning_impact: If disrupted or avoided, Asia-Europe traffic may be rerouted around the Cape of Good Hope, adding major transit time and fuel/cost exposure.
chokepoints.readiness_action: If route depends on Suez/Red Sea, check current Suez Canal Authority circulars, carrier route advisories, and security notices before confirming readiness.
chokepoints.hard_gate_condition: Only hard gate if live authority/carrier/security advisory confirms closure, prohibition, or carrier refusal for the intended sailing.

chokepoints.name: Panama Canal draft and transit exposure
chokepoints.typical_planning_impact: Draft and daily transit-slot changes can force booking delays, lighter loading, alternate routing, or higher transit cost exposure.
chokepoints.readiness_action: If route depends on Panama Canal, check current Panama Canal Authority advisories for maximum authorized draft, booking slots, and transit restrictions.
chokepoints.hard_gate_condition: Hard gate only if current advisory shows the vessel/cargo plan cannot transit under current draft/slot rules.

chokepoints.name: Strait of Malacca exposure
chokepoints.typical_planning_impact: High-density Asia trade corridor; disruption or congestion can affect schedule reliability.
chokepoints.readiness_action: Flag as strategic chokepoint exposure for Asia-Europe/Asia-Middle East routings; validate carrier ETA and schedule buffer.
chokepoints.hard_gate_condition: Hard gate only if live authority/carrier/security advisory confirms closure or sailing restriction.

chokepoints.name: Bab-el-Mandeb / Red Sea exposure
chokepoints.typical_planning_impact: Security incidents can cause carrier avoidance of Red Sea/Suez route and rerouting via Cape of Good Hope.
chokepoints.readiness_action: For routes using Red Sea/Suez, validate current carrier routing and security advisories before confirming timeline.
chokepoints.hard_gate_condition: Hard gate only when carrier refuses the route or live security/authority notices block transit.

chokepoints.name: Strait of Hormuz exposure
chokepoints.typical_planning_impact: Strategic energy and Gulf trade chokepoint; disruption can affect insurance, carrier acceptance, and service reliability.
chokepoints.readiness_action: For Gulf cargo, validate carrier acceptance, insurance/security advisories, and schedule buffer.
chokepoints.hard_gate_condition: Hard gate only when carrier, authority, or security advisory blocks intended transit.

schedule_readiness_rules.name: Cargo / container gate-in cutoff
schedule_readiness_rules.applies_when: containerized ocean shipment has assigned sailing or vessel booking
schedule_readiness_rules.default_deadline_assumption: carrier/terminal specific; commonly days before sailing, do not infer exact time without schedule source
schedule_readiness_rules.hard_gate_condition: container_ready_datetime or terminal_gate_in_time is after gate_in_cutoff_datetime
schedule_readiness_rules.readiness_message: Container misses terminal gate-in cutoff; it cannot be loaded on that sailing unless carrier/terminal grants exception.

schedule_readiness_rules.name: VGM cutoff
schedule_readiness_rules.applies_when: packed export container subject to SOLAS VGM
schedule_readiness_rules.default_deadline_assumption: must be submitted before the carrier/terminal VGM cutoff and before vessel loading
schedule_readiness_rules.hard_gate_condition: VGM missing or submitted after VGM cutoff
schedule_readiness_rules.readiness_message: No timely VGM means no load for that sailing.

schedule_readiness_rules.name: Shipping instructions / documentation cutoff
schedule_readiness_rules.applies_when: ocean shipment requires bill of lading or sea waybill generation
schedule_readiness_rules.default_deadline_assumption: carrier/sailing specific; must be validated per booking
schedule_readiness_rules.hard_gate_condition: shipping instructions missing or submitted after carrier documentation cutoff
schedule_readiness_rules.readiness_message: Late shipping instructions can block B/L creation and cause shipment roll or documentation failure.

schedule_readiness_rules.name: Dangerous goods document cutoff
schedule_readiness_rules.default_deadline_assumption: carrier/sailing specific; often earlier than standard documentation cutoff
schedule_readiness_rules.hard_gate_condition: DG declaration or required DG packing certificate missing/late
schedule_readiness_rules.readiness_message: DG paperwork must be accepted before cargo can be planned for vessel stowage.

schedule_readiness_rules.name: Missed service-cycle impact
schedule_readiness_rules.default_deadline_assumption: if service_frequency_days unknown, weekly container service estimate = 7 days with low confidence
schedule_readiness_rules.hard_gate_condition: none; missing a sailing is a delay/cost planning factor unless cargo has a fixed latest acceptable arrival date
schedule_readiness_rules.readiness_message: Missing a weekly ocean service usually loses a service cycle, not a few hours.

schedule_readiness_rules.name: Demurrage / terminal free-time risk
schedule_readiness_rules.applies_when: full import container remains in terminal beyond free time
schedule_readiness_rules.default_deadline_assumption: free time and tariff are carrier/country/terminal specific
schedule_readiness_rules.hard_gate_condition: none by default; may become operational block if charges/holds prevent release
schedule_readiness_rules.readiness_message: Pickup after free time expiry can trigger demurrage/storage charges.

schedule_readiness_rules.name: Detention / equipment free-time risk
schedule_readiness_rules.applies_when: empty container is returned after agreed free time
schedule_readiness_rules.default_deadline_assumption: free time and tariff are carrier/country/terminal specific
schedule_readiness_rules.hard_gate_condition: none by default; may become cost or equipment-release issue
schedule_readiness_rules.readiness_message: Late empty return can trigger detention charges.
